project.name: SALIS AUTO
project.description: World-class automotive ERP / Garage Management System (Saudi-Arabia compliant) with a React client, Express/Node server, and shared Drizzle ORM PostgreSQL schema.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion list that prevents node_modules, local env files, and build artifacts from being copied into the image.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose orchestration defining the SALIS AUTO application service alongside a database service with a persistent named volume.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build that compiles the app in a node:20-alpine builder stage and produces a lean production runner image exposing port 5000.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build copies the built dist and node_modules from the builder stage to keep the runtime image small.

nodes.name: test.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs the project test suite on push and pull request events.

nodes.name: 0001_cultured_apocalypse.sql
nodes.summary: Initial Drizzle migration defining the core SALIS AUTO ERP schema with 222 tables covering customers, vehicles, job cards, invoicing, inventory, scheduling, and AI features.
nodes.languageNotes: Drizzle-generated PostgreSQL migration; foreign keys and constraints define inter-table relationships.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Begin with the README to understand SALIS AUTO: an Automotive ERP and Garage Management System tailored for the Saudi market. It covers the product's purpose, Saudi-specific features (VAT/ZATCA e-invoicing, Hijri dates), the React + Express + Drizzle/PostgreSQL stack, and the high-level module map. This document is the roadmap for the rest of the tour.

tour.title: Project Manifest & Build Config
tour.description: The package.json declares the application's scripts (dev, build, start, db push) and the full dependency set spanning the React client, Express server, and shared Drizzle schema. The tsconfig.json ties client, server, and shared code together under one TypeScript configuration. Together they define how the monorepo is built and run.

tour.title: Shared Database Schema
tour.description: The shared Drizzle ORM schema is the foundation the entire system depends on (one of the highest fan-in files in the codebase). It defines every database table, relation, and TypeScript type, giving the backend and frontend a single source of truth for the data model. Understanding this vocabulary first makes every later layer easier to follow.
tour.languageLesson: Drizzle ORM defines tables in TypeScript and derives static types via helpers like InferSelectModel, so the same schema produces both the SQL structure and the compile-time types shared across client and server.

tour.title: The Database Migration
tour.description: This initial Drizzle migration materializes the schema from Step 3 into PostgreSQL, defining over 200 tables covering customers, vehicles, job cards, invoicing, inventory, scheduling, and AI features. It is the physical persistence layer the schema types map onto. The breadth of tables here reflects the ERP's wide functional surface.
tour.languageLesson: SQL migrations are applied in lexical order and should be idempotent and reversible, letting the database structure evolve in lock-step with the application code.

tour.title: Server Entry Point
tour.description: server/index.ts is the backend's entry point and the start of the dependency graph: it bootstraps the Express app, registers routes, and starts the HTTP server. From here the BFS traversal fans out into configuration, database wiring, authentication, and the route layer. This is 'how the backend starts.'

tour.title: Database & Storage Layer
tour.description: Building on the schema from Step 3, server/db.ts wires Drizzle ORM to the PostgreSQL connection pool and exports the db client, while server/storage.ts is the central data-access repository exposing CRUD operations over every entity. Almost all backend business logic flows through this storage layer, keeping SQL access in one place.

tour.title: Authentication & Access Control
tour.description: server/auth.ts provides password hashing, session management, Passport setup, and route-guard middleware that protects the API. The RBAC configuration and middleware layer enforce role-based permissions across the multi-role ERP (admin, technician, customer, purchase agent). These guards wrap the route handlers introduced next.

tour.title: API Routing Layer
tour.description: Requests are dispatched through two complementary mechanisms: server/routes/index.ts aggregates and mounts the modular feature routers, while the legacy server/routes.ts still defines a large block of endpoints directly. server/routes.ts has very high fan-out, reflecting how much of the API surface it touches. Auth routes illustrate the typical feature-router pattern.

tour.title: Saudi Compliance Services
tour.description: A defining feature of SALIS AUTO is Saudi-market compliance. These backend services implement ZATCA Phase-2 e-invoicing and broader Saudi tax/regulatory logic that the invoicing and reporting routes rely on. They turn the generic ERP into a locally compliant product, complementing the Hijri/VAT features mentioned in the README.

tour.title: Workflow Engine & Real-Time
tour.description: The engine modules orchestrate automation: a workflow engine coordinates triggers, state machines, and scheduled checks, exposed through an engine barrel module. server/websocket.ts manages real-time client connections and event broadcasting for live updates (tracking, notifications, chat). Together they drive the event-driven and automated behavior across the ERP.

tour.title: Frontend Entry & App Shell
tour.description: Switching to the client: index.html mounts the single-page app, main.tsx bootstraps React into the DOM, and App.tsx is the root component that wires client-side routing (wouter) for every feature page and layout. App.tsx has the highest fan-out on the frontend, making it the map of the entire UI.
tour.languageLesson: React SPAs render from a single root mounted in index.html; client-side routers like wouter then swap page components by URL without full-page reloads.

tour.title: Frontend Data, Auth & Navigation
tour.description: Cross-cutting client infrastructure ties the UI to the backend: lib/queryClient.ts configures TanStack Query for server-state fetching/caching (very high fan-in), the useAuth hook encapsulates session state, and config/navigation.ts defines the role-aware menu structure. The shared Layout component provides the navigation chrome and sidebar around feature pages.
tour.languageLesson: React hooks (use*) and a centralized query client let components subscribe to server state declaratively, with caching and refetching handled outside the component tree.

tour.title: Shared UI Component Library
tour.description: The shadcn/ui primitives (button, card, input, dialog, select) are the most depended-upon files in the whole project by fan-in. Built on TailwindCSS, they form the consistent design system that every page composes. Recognizing these primitives makes the 250+ feature pages quick to read.

tour.title: Representative Feature Pages
tour.description: With the shell, data layer, and components understood, these pages show the patterns in action: Customers and Technician Management are high-fan-out CRUD screens, while the Saudi Compliance Dashboard surfaces the ZATCA/VAT logic from Step 9 in the UI. They are templates for how the rest of the ERP's screens are built.

tour.title: Containerization & CI
tour.description: Finally, the infrastructure ties it all together. A multi-stage Dockerfile compiles the app in a node:20-alpine builder and ships a lean production image on port 5000; docker-compose orchestrates that app service alongside a PostgreSQL database with a persistent volume. The GitHub Actions workflow runs the test suite on every push and pull request.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds keep dev/build dependencies out of the final image, while docker-compose's depends_on and named volumes manage service startup order and persistent data.
